authorMeta.name: zevialover2000
text: Choose wisely what you consume on social media. Follow people that are authentic, that are actually helpful, and REALISTIC in their gym content. Constantly saving mirror body check videos from influencers won’t get you any closer to your goals. And feeling good shouldn’t be unrealistic to maintain. It should fit your life, your needs, and your schedule. And if all you can do is give the bare minimum right now, that’s better than not giving anything at all. Don’t let social media fool you into thinking that you need to meet unrealistic standards. #fyp #gymtok #fitness #health #coach

authorMeta.name: thelosttwo
text:
-follow thelosttwo for more travel inspo ✨
Is 🇵🇼 the most beautiful country in the world?
Welcome to Palau, a place that can only be described as something from another planet 👽🌎
Here, you’ll find towering limestone islands shaped like mushrooms, rising straight out of crystal-clear turquoise waters. These surreal seascapes aren’t just beautiful, they’re protected. In fact, fishing is banned in 80% of the country, making Palau one of the best diving destinations on Earth 🤿🐠🦈
Vibrant coral reefs, manta rays, reef sharks, and sea turtles, the marine life here is everywhere. And with no hotels in its most iconic areas, much of it remains untouched by mass tourism.
If you’re looking for a unique destination to add to your bucketlist, you NEED to put Palau at the very top!
TAG someone that you would bring here 👇🏼

authorMeta.name: themissingself
text: 🧬GENDER ARGUMENT 🧜‍♂️ | It’s hard to believe how divisive the topic of gender has become. The plight of the of trans* community, to feel safe in their bodies, homes, and country, has somehow turned into the most controversial issue of our time. You’d think the attention on the issue would breed good conversation, but no. NOWHERE, across the entirety of the internet, could I find a reasonable debate about gender. That is why I made this video a bit longer than the others, I felt a sense of responsibility to capture the essence of what people on both sides would say to each other if they weren’t so angry all the time. I feel so much compassion towards the trans community, trying to lead a movement of unprecedented inclusion, and I also feel for all of the people that are confused by the incompleteness of its ideology. It’s ironic that the debate is about identity, considering the fact that both sides seem to have forgotten that they are not their identities. The real battle always seems to be waging within ourselves, between the self that has no description, and the costume that it wears so that others can describe us. I hope it doesn’t take too much v10/ence for all of us to see ourselves in each-other. | #gender #transition #identity  #themissingself #educational #debate #inclusion #man #woman #nonbinary

text:
My Roth IRA After 8 Years of Consistency!

Most people would leave this out… but, this did not come from me magically 5X’ing my Roth IRA contributions by picking perfect stocks.

A big part of this balance came from rolling over old Roth 401Ks from previous employers.

The actual strategy has been boring:

Max it when I can.
Buy low-cost broad-market ETFs.
Stay invested.
Roll old retirement accounts forward.
Repeat.

Not flashy. Not complicated. But it works.

How old were you when you opened your Roth IRA?

#RothIRA #Investing #WealthBuilding #PersonalFinance #FinancialLiteracy

It's paydayyy... this is exactly how I budget my paycheck 💸

If you want to get better at money this year, I just launched a FREE mini-course that covers all the personal finance basics about mindset, budgeting, the exact accounts and tools I use, and the money system I used to go from $0 to $1m in net worth in under 7 years. Click the link to join for free!

To answer a few common questions:

I work in strategy for an energy company. We help define and execute the company's most strategic, highest risk, and highest investment projects like real estate transactions, mergers and acquisitions, divestments, software implementations, etc.

I have a bachelors and a masters degree in business.

I don't have any debt other than my mortgages.

I max out all of my retirement accounts:
- HSA (my employer contributes $1k on my behalf)
- 401k (I had a big bonus earlier this year so my contributions are lower)
- Roth (I make over the limit so I do a conversion)

This is not my only income. I also own a rental property and earn some money from social media (not a lot yet though!). This is just the routine I do 2x a month after getting paid from my corporate job.

I'm not living paycheck to paycheck, I use "zero-based" budgeting 😄

All my personal finance educational resources are free because I believe health money habits are for everyone at any age, on any income, and personal finance education should be accessible to all.

I appreicate all the DMs asking me out on a date but I am very happily partnered, I just don't show him much online for our own privacy. It's flattering though!

text:
The most common ISA mistake in the UK!

Most people who have an ISA think they're doing the right thing. They're saving every month. They're being responsible. They're building something for the future.

But there's one decision, one that takes less than five minutes, that separates the people who build real wealth from the people who save diligently for 25 years and still end up with half as much.

In this video we follow two twins. Same salary. Same £200 a month. Same 25 years. One ends up with £163,000. The other ends up with £82,000. The difference has nothing to do with how much they saved - and everything to do with what they put inside their ISA.

This is the most common ISA mistake in the UK. And most people making it have absolutely no idea.

⚠️ This video is for educational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research before making any financial decisions.

authorMeta.name: recipeincaption
text:
Sheet Pan Garlic Parmesan Chicken and Potatoes 🧄🧈

For ALL of you asking about the app, search “Osta” on the app or Google play store 🫶

Minimal effort, maximum flavor… juicy chicken, crispy potatoes covered in garlicky parmesan butter perfection 🔥

For the potatoes:
- 1.5 lbs (680 g) baby potatoes
- 1/3 cup butter, melted
- 1 cup parmesan cheese
- 8 garlic cloves, minced
- Seasonings: 2 tsp each of paprika, oregano, garlic powder, salt, black pepper
- 3 tbsp olive oil

For the chicken:
- 6 to 8 chicken thighs, boneless & skinless
- Seasonings: 1 tbsp seasoning salt, 1 tbsp paprika, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp onion powder, 2 tsp italian seasoning, 1 tsp cumin
- 1 lemon, juiced
- 2 tbsp mayonnaise

For the garlic & onion:
- 2 garlic heads + 2 tbsp olive oil + 2 tsp salt
- 1 red onion, cut into chunks + olive oil, drizzled

For the Aioli: 
- 1/3 cup greek yogurt + 1/3 cup mayonnaise
- 2 roasted garlic heads
- 1/4 lemon, juiced
- 2 tsp black pepper + 1 tsp salt
- 3 tbsp chives, chopped

For the Baste (optional ):
- 1/4 cup mayonnaise 
- 1/4 cup parmesan 
- 2 tsp paprika

Instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the chicken, seasonings, lemon juice, garlic, and mayonnaise. Mix well and marinate for as long as you wish (up to overnight).
2. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Add the potatoes to a large casserole dish. Toss with the melted butter, parmesan, olive oil, garlic, and seasonings. Spread evenly.
3. Top with the chicken thighs. Add the red onion chunks around the dish & drizzle them with olive oil. 
4. Slice the tops off the garlic heads, drizzle with olive oil and salt, wrap in foil, & place in dish.
5. Bake for 40 mins. Broil for the last 2–3 mins if desired. 
6. Optional but recommended: Mix together mayo, parmesan, and paprika. At the 20-min mark, baste the chicken & return to the oven.
7. In a bowl, combine the yogurt, mayo, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chives. Squeeze in as much of the roasted garlic as you wish OR chop them up first. Mix.
8. Finish with fresh parm, chives, & lemon slices. Enjoy!

#recipe #cookingathometiktoktv #dinner #recipesoftiktok #dinnerideas

authorMeta.name: brittydcook
text:
You HAVE to try this Mapo Tofu from My Hero Academia! Bakugo nailed it

Comment your thoughts on MHA and follow for more!

Recipe
- 1lb ground pork
- 2-3 inch knob ginger
- 6 cloves garlic 
- 6 Birds Eye chilies 
- 6 whole green onions
- 1-2 tbsp ground Szechuan peppercorns
- 1/3 cup shaoxing wine
- 1/4 cup soy sauce 
- 2 cups chicken stock
- I like to use medium tofu for this. I know some people do silken but I like a little more texture 
- 4 tbsp doubanjiang
- 2 tbsp douchi
- 2-3 tbsp sugar
- 2 big pinches of salt and msg
- 1tbsp cornstarch slurry
- 1/4 cup lard

Start by prepping your vegetables. Grate all of the ginger and garlic. Reserve half of the ginger for marinating the pork. Add ginger salt pepper and a splash each of soy sauce and shaoxing wine. Knead to combine. 
Brown the pork in a wok then add in all of your vegetables, saute. Add in your pastes and peppercorns. Cook for a couple more minutes before adding chicken stock, soy sauce and shaoxing wine. Season here. I like to reduce this down by about 1/3 before adding the slurry. Allow to thicken then add in your tofu and green onions tops. Serve over rice and enjoy!

@YOSUKATA Wok

authorMeta.name: mariahhcooks
text:
Hot Honey Chicken Pasta 🍯🔥 the viral recipe with over 60+ million views, 20/10 hits every time!

Recipe:

Chicken Tenders
• 1 lb chicken tenderloins
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp seasoned salt
• 1 tsp creole seasoning
• 1 tsp cayenne pepper (optional, adjust to spice)
• 1 tsp all purpose seasoning
• 1 sazon packet
• 1–2 cups buttermilk
• ⅛ cup jalapeño or pickle juice
• 3 cups all-purpose flour (season with same seasonings)

Hot Honey Sauce
• ½ cup buffalo sauce
• ¼ cup honey
• ⅛ cup hot sauce (optional)

Pasta
• 1 lb penne pasta
• 2 tbsp unsalted butter
• 2 garlic cloves, finely minced
• ½ yellow onion, finely chopped
• 1 ½ cups heavy cream
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp seasoned salt
• 1 tsp creole seasoning
• 1 tsp cayenne pepper (optional)
• 1 tsp all purpose seasoning
• 1 tsp black pepper
• Grated mozzarella
• Grated Parmesan

Instructions
1. Season chicken tenderloins add in buttermilk and jalapeño/pickle juice then marinate for at least 30 minutes to an hour. 
2. Coat chicken in seasoned flour set aside on wire rack and rest for 10 minutes before frying. Fry at 350°F until golden brown and crispy. Set aside and let cool on a wire rack.
3. In a sauce pan, add 1 tbsp of unsalted butter, buffalo sauce, honey, and hot sauce and simmer until fully combined. Then toss chicken in the hot honey sauce.
4. In a skillet, melt butter and sauté garlic and onion until fragrant. Pour in heavy cream and stir in all seasonings.
5. Add mozzarella and Parmesan until melted and creamy, then toss in cooked pasta.
6. Top creamy pasta with hot honey chicken and extra sauce if desired and enjoy :)

#hothoneychickenpasta #hothoney #chickenpasta #pasta #fypシ

text:
Crockpot Chicken Pasta

Ingredients

• 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken breasts
• 1 tsp salt
• 1 tsp black pepper
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1 tbsp Kinder’s Buttery Garlic & Herb Seasoning
• 1 jar marinara sauce (24 oz)
• 1 jar Alfredo sauce (15 oz)
• 1 block cream cheese (8 oz)
• 3 cloves garlic, minced
• 1 bag shredded mozzarella cheese (8 oz)
• Parsley flakes, for garnish

Instructions

1. Add the chicken breasts to the crockpot and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and Kinder’s Buttery Garlic & Herb Seasoning.
2. Pour in the marinara sauce and Alfredo sauce. Add the cream cheese and minced garlic, then gently mix everything together.
3. Cover and cook on HIGH for 3 hours, or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender.
4. Remove the chicken, shred it with two forks, and return it to the crockpot.
5. Add the shredded mozzarella cheese and stir until the cheese is melted and everything is creamy and combined.
6. Top with parsley flakes and serve.

#crockpotrecipes #chickenpasta #slowcookerrecipes #easyrecipes #comfortfood

authorMeta.name: .cookwithbrooke
text:
Sheet Pan Chicken Taco Lettuce Boats

I forget how ridiculously good this recipe is every single time I make it. Crispy taco chicken, chili-lime street corn, all the cold toppings, and somehow you still feel like a functioning adult afterward instead of needing a nap.

And yes, I used queso fresco. Mind your business.                    Ingredients

For the chicken:

* 2½ lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, chopped small
* 2 packets taco seasoning
* 1 teaspoon garlic powder
* 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
* Juice of 1 lime

For the chili-lime corn:

* 4 fresh ears of corn, kernels shaved off
* ½ purple onion, diced
* 1 tablespoon olive oil
* 1 teaspoon chili powder
* Salt to taste
* Juice of ½–1 lime
* ½ cup queso fresco
* Fresh cilantro, chopped

For serving:

* Green leaf lettuce (sandwich cut)
* Diced avocado
* Pico de gallo
* Jalapeño slices
* Crema or Greek yogurt
* Extra cilantro

Instructions

1. Preheat oven to 425°F and line a sheet pan with parchment paper.
2. Toss chopped chicken with taco seasoning, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and lime juice.
3. Spread chicken onto one side of the sheet pan in an even layer. No oil needed.
4. Bake for 10 minutes.
5. Toss corn and purple onion with olive oil, chili powder, and salt.
6. Remove the pan, push chicken to one side, and add the corn mixture to the empty side.
7. Return to oven for another 10 minutes, then broil the last 3 minutes for crispy edges and charred corn.
8. Remove from oven and squeeze lime juice over the corn. Top with queso fresco and cilantro.
9. Assemble lettuce boats with chicken, street corn, avocado, pico, jalapeños, crema, and extra cilantro #taco #dinner #recipe #sheetpandinner #chicken

Home Cooking Sessions: Thai Beef Salad with the most incredible lemongrass dressing. Full recipe and long-form video now up on Substack, link in bio 🇹🇭😍
-
For me, there are very few salads that compare to this one, and a lot of that comes down to the dressing. It’s sharp, sweet, salty and spicy, with layers of heat, citrus and umami that somehow all work together perfectly. It’s incredibly versatile too, so once you make the dressing, you can pair it with all sorts of proteins. 

I used lemongrass in both the dressing and butter baste to bring freshness and that really fragrant citrus note through the beef. The toasted rice powder is another key element here - it adds nuttiness, texture and body whilst helping absorb excess moisture so the salad stays crisp.

There’s plenty of crushing, grinding and layering of flavours in this one, but if you don’t have a mortar and pestle, a food processor works perfectly well too. Full recipe, ingredients and long-form video now live on Substack, link in bio. NF x

authorMeta.name: recipeincaption
text:
Crispy Chicken Shawarma Wraps 🔥

For all of you asking about my recipe organizing app, search “Osta” on the app or google play store 🫶

Juicy chicken shawarma, creamy garlic sauce and crispy fries wrapped into one perfect bite 🌯

For the Chicken: -1.5 lbs (680g) skinless chicken thighs, sliced into strips -Seasoning: 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp onion powder, 2 tsp coriander, 1 tsp allspice, 2 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp salt -3 tbsp olive oil -8 garlic cloves, minced -2 tbsp tomato paste -1/3 cup Greek yogurt -½ lemon, juiced

For the Onions: -1 red onion, thinly sliced -2 tbsp olive oil -½ lemon, juiced -1/4 cup fresh parsley, finely chopped -1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 2 tsp sumac

For the Garlic Sauce: -1/2 cup Greek yogurt -1/2 cup mayonnaise -3 garlic cloves, minced -1/4 lemon, juiced

For the Red Sauce Glaze: -1/4 cup olive oil + 2 tbsp tomato paste -1 tbsp pomegranate molasses (optional) -1 tsp paprika

For Assembly: -3 Roma tomatoes, diced -Pitas -Frozen french fries

Instructions:

1. In a bowl, combine the sliced chicken, spices, olive oil, garlic, tomato paste, yogurt, and lemon juice. Mix well & marinate for as long as your patience can handle, up to overnight.
2. In a large pan over medium-high heat, add a layer of olive oil. Once hot, add the chicken. Cook undisturbed for 3 mins, then cook for 6–7 more mins, stirring often, until golden & cooked through.
3. In a bowl, combine the sliced red onion, olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, salt, pepper, and sumac. Toss well, set aside.
4. In a bowl, mix the Greek yogurt, mayo, garlic, and lemon juice. Set aside in fridge.
5. In a bowl, whisk olive oil, tomato paste, pomegranate molasses (if using), & paprika.
6. Prepare your French fries however you like. Assemble the wraps: spread a generous layer of garlic sauce onto each pita. Top with  chicken, French fries, diced tomatoes & the onions. Wrap tightly.
7. Melt butter in a pan over low heat. Place the wraps seam-side down & brush the tops with the red sauce. Cook until crispy golden, flip, brush the other side, & repeat. Top with more garlic sauce. Enjoy!!

text:
Honey BBQ Chicken Tenders 🍯

Level up a boring cookout with these chicken tenders using @Sweet Baby Ray’s to make a sticky + sweet honey bbq glaze 😋 The sauce takes these to another level, so don’t skip it 🙂‍↔️

👉 full detailed recipes in bio 🔗

—

RECIPE:

Chicken (Dry Brine):
6–8 tenders tossed with kosher salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika. Rest uncovered in the fridge for at least 2 hours (overnight is best).

Seasoned Flour + Wet Batter:
Mix AP flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cayenne, black pepper + pinch MSG. Move ~⅓ to another bowl and whisk with cold sparkling water until thin pancake batter.

Double Dredge:
Mix 1–2 tbsp wet batter into dry flour for craggly bits. Coat chicken: wet → dry → wet → dry (press to stick).

Fry:
350°F oil for 4–6 mins until golden + CWISPY (and 165F internal).

Honey BBQ Glaze:
Heat BBQ sauce (Sweet Baby Ray’s), honey, butter, vinegar, hot sauce until smooth + glossy. Season to taste. Toss or brush chicken with glaze while it’s still hot.

Finish:
Top with herbs, an extra drizzle of sauce, & serve with ranch. Enjoy friends :) 

—

Recipe:

Servings: 2-4 People
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 1 hour

Steak:
1-2 Large Steaks of your choosing (ideally Bone-In Ribeye)
Unsalted Butter, for finish
Canola Oil, as needed
Outback Rub:
2.5 tbsp Kosher Salt
1 tbsp Black Pepper

1 tsp Onion Powder
½ tsp Chili Powder
½ tsp Brown Sugar
Pinch of Cayenne
Serving:
Grilled Shrimp (optional)
Loaded Baked Potato
Steamed Broccoli
Instructions:
Begin by generously letting your steaks come to room temperature for 30-40 minutes. Then slather in oil and generously season your steaks with the Outback Rub.
Preheat your grill to high heat (about 400F).
Add the steaks to the grill and sear off for about 3-4 minutes per side or until 120F internal. Once done, pull off, top with butter and let the steaks rest for 8 minutes.
Serve the steaks with a loaded baked potato and steamed broccoli on the side. Make sure to start with the Outback Bloomin Onion too! Serve and enjoy!

authorMeta.name: tacticalarchives_
text: M249 SAW Gunner in a Heavy Firefight in Afghanistan | No Copyright Intended |               The M249 SAW. 800 rounds per minute of sustained suppressive fire carried by one man into some of the most hostile terrain on earth. Afghanistan was tailor made for the SAW gunner; wide open valleys, compounds at distance and ambushes that could come from any ridgeline at any moment. The gunner’s job was simple in theory and brutal in practice; lay down enough fire to keep the enemy’s heads down while your team maneuvered and your medic reached the wounded. In the Korengal Valley, Helmand Province and the mountain passes of Kunar, SAW gunners burned through belt after belt in contacts that lasted hours under a sun that pushed 45 degrees. The weapon weighs 7 kilograms unloaded; with a full 200 round belt, kit and body armour a SAW gunner in Afghanistan was carrying close to 50 kilograms up mountain terrain at altitude. Every step earned. Every trigger pull deliberate. The men behind the M249 were the backbone of every infantry squad that ever set foot in that country and the footage they left behind is some of the most visceral and raw documentation of modern combat ever recorded…                  Source: Funker530 #TacticalArchives #fyp #afghanistan #war #military

authorMeta.name: historyfootage.com
text:
In the final days of the Battle of Berlin, Germany was facing complete military collapse as the Soviet Red Army closed in on the capital from multiple directions.

With most experienced frontline troops already lost or reassigned, the defense of Berlin increasingly relied on a mixture of remaining regular army units, police formations, and hastily organized civilian militias. 
Among these was the Volkssturm, created in late 1944 as a last-resort mobilization of males considered fit for service, including older men and teenagers who had received only minimal military training.

The city’s defenders were placed in extremely difficult conditions. 
Supply shortages, destroyed infrastructure, and continuous artillery and aerial bombardment severely reduced their ability to coordinate an effective defense. Communication between units was often disrupted, and command structures became increasingly fragmented as the situation worsened.

Despite these limitations, fighting continued within the urban environment, with combat taking place block by block. 
The defensive effort combined remnants of organized military resistance with improvised units, reflecting the rapidly deteriorating strategic situation in the final phase of the war in Europe.
#germany #berlin #ww2 #historytok #historyfootage

In 1946, Karl Hermann reached his final day after being convicted of war crimes.

As one of the top Nazi officials in occupied Czechoslovakia, he was deeply involved in violent crackdowns on resistance movements, including the brutal reprisals that followed the assassination of Reinhard Heydrich. 

On May 22, 1946, in Prague, Frank was publicly executed before a large crowd. Unlike the battlefield deaths often associated with the war, his end came through legal judgment and accountability.

His execution marked more than just the fall of one man, it reflected a broader post-war shift, where former figures of power were being held responsible for their actions.

authorMeta.name: animalstoryai3
text:
She woke up before me.

I didn't know.

I just felt something —
the softest, most gentle thing —
against my cheek.

Her tiny hand.

And then the quietest little voice
right beside my ear:

"Mamma."

Not crying.
Not calling loudly.
Just...
telling me she was there.
Telling me she loved me.
In the only word she knows. 🥹

That hug.
That tiny body pressed into mine.
Those little arms around my neck.

I have been loved before.
But nothing —
nothing —
has ever felt like this. 💛🏠

Save this for every mom who has 
had a morning like this 🥹

Tag who you want to wake up 
next to 💕

authorMeta.name: runx2studio
text:
Having spent the past months producing AI short dramas with Seedance 2.0 + Fast, I’ve been able to observe the market dynamics firsthand—and the insights are fascinating:

* Rapid Production, Low Cost – A 2-minute episode can now be produced in roughly 10 days, using minimal compute (~200 seconds per episode). This efficiency challenges traditional production timelines and budgets.
* Market Imbalance – Globally, AI-driven short dramas are gaining traction. Yet in the UK, local production is almost non-existent. Platforms are largely unprepared for this wave, creating a clear opportunity for early movers.
* The Real Bottleneck: Storytelling – Technology is no longer the limiting factor. High-quality scripts remain scarce, and audience engagement depends heavily on narrative depth.
* Strategic Opportunity – For creators, studios, and platforms, the combination of human creativity + AI efficiency can unlock scalable, rapid, and cost-effective storytelling. Those who bridge the gap between strong narratives and AI production will define the next era of digital entertainment.

I’m actively exploring collaborations with writers, creative teams, and innovative platforms to push the boundaries of AI-generated storytelling. The future of short-form entertainment is hybrid—and it’s happening now.

#AI #ShortDrama #DigitalStorytelling #CreativeTech #ContentInnovation

Native plants don’t have to look wild or messy 🌿✨
The secret to a beautiful native landscape isn’t avoiding native plants — it’s thoughtful garden design.
From structured pollinator beds and clean edging to layered textures and year-round interest, native landscaping can look polished, elegant, and intentional while supporting local ecosystems right here in Pennsylvania.
Featuring Pennsylvania native plants like:
🌼 Coneflowers
🌼 Black-eyed Susans
🌾 Switchgrass
🌿 Inkberry Holly
Native gardens help support bees, butterflies, and pollinators while reducing water use, fertilizer needs, and long-term landscape maintenance. Sustainable landscaping can absolutely be beautiful landscaping.
Whether you love formal garden design, modern landscaping, cottage gardens, pollinator gardens, or low maintenance outdoor spaces, native plants can create a landscape that’s both stunning and environmentally friendly.
Visit Main Line Gardens in Malvern for native plants, landscape inspiration, pollinator garden ideas, sustainable landscaping, garden design tips, and expert planting advice.

authorMeta.name: gardening_ana
text:
Five plants that separate a designed garden from a planted one.
Rosemary — most people treat it as a herb. Designers use it as a structural evergreen with silver foliage, late winter bloom, and serious drought tolerance once established.
Echinops — steel-blue globe thistle that holds its form into winter. Pollinators love it, it seeds around just enough, and nothing else gives you that texture.
White Cloud Nepeta — all the soft billowing habit of catmint, none of the blue. Cleaner in a white or silver palette. Underused because most people don’t know it exists.
Mountain Hydrangea (Hydrangea serrata) — more refined than arborescens, lacecap form, better fall color, and it actually performs in part shade without flopping.
Blue Mist Fothergilla (Fothergilla gardenii ‘Blue Mist’) — blue-green foliage all season, then flame in fall. Native, compact, and genuinely beautiful. The plant that makes clients ask “what is that?”
#gardendesign #landscapedesign

authorMeta.name: landspace.arch
text:
Landscape architecture terms you should know —
Water & Climate Edition 

1️⃣ Bio-swale
A planted channel that slows and filters stormwater

2️⃣ Rain garden
A shallow landscape that collects and absorbs rain

3️⃣ Retention vs Detention basin
Retention holds water permanently
Detention stores and releases it slowly

4️⃣ Permeable pavement
Lets water pass through instead of running off

5️⃣ Green roof
A vegetated roof that manages water and reduces heat

6️⃣ Blue-green infrastructure
Where water systems and landscape design work together

Understanding water is key, great landscape design starts with how water moves.

#landscapearchitecture #landscapedesign #landscapearchitecturestudent

authorMeta.name: mio.travel
text:
The Romans spent 250 years turning a Spanish mountain into Mars 🏛️

This is for travellers drawn to ancient engineering, landscapes that don't look real, and history nobody talks about.

📍 Las Médulas - El Bierzo, León, Spain

Roman engineers called the technique ruina montium ("the ruin of mountains"): they dug tunnels into the hillside, built 100km of aqueducts to channel water in, then released it all at once. The pressure shattered the rock from the inside out, and the gold-bearing debris washed below.

Here are 4 tips for visiting Las Médulas:
1. About an hour from León or Ponferrada. Easiest as a Camino de Santiago detour or part of a Castilla y León road trip.
2. The Mirador de Orellán viewpoint is the iconic panorama. Go at sunset for the best light on the red spires.
3. Walk through the Cuevona and the Galería de Orellán. Roman-cut tunnels where you can still see the original water channels carved into the stone.
4. The visitor centre in Carucedo explains the ruina montium technique with working models. 

Send this video to the mio app and it'll save Las Médulas straight onto your travel map.

#LasMedulas #spainhikes #UNESCO #SpainTravel #ancientengineering

authorMeta.name: hunter.boudreaux1
text: Europe’s underrated gem 🇪🇺 If you want stunning views without spending like you would in Paris, Montenegro could genuinely catch you off guard. This compact country is filled with historic towns, ancient stone castles, clear blue waters, and breathtaking mountain scenery that almost doesn’t feel real. Spots like Kotor Old Town look like they belong in a film, with tight alleyways and deep-rooted history around every corner. Along the shoreline, you’ll come across pristine beaches and beautiful coastal roads by the Adriatic, delivering picture-perfect scenery in nearly every direction. On top of that, compared to much of Western Europe, it’s quite budget-friendly. Meals, places to stay, and getting around usually cost less—especially if you avoid peak travel periods. Entry requirements vary depending on your passport, so it’s always smart to confirm before making plans, but many visitors can enter without much hassle for short visits. Montenegro isn’t only affordable… it’s peaceful, stunning, and still flying under the radar. Sometimes the most memorable destinations aren’t the most popular… they’re the ones people haven’t fully discovered yet. 💬 Drop a 🌍 if this just made your travel list. . . Join us on our journey ✨#travelinspo #fyp #travel #europeansummer

authorMeta.name: dana_conway
text:
Settle down now, school is in session😏 Welcome back to “Single, but cooking like someone’s falling for me tonight.” Who’s hungry?👀

GIANT GARLIC BREAD GRILLED HOT DOGS 🌭🧄🧀

For the hot dog rolls:
• 500 g strong white bread flour
• 10 g salt
• 10 g caster sugar
• 7 g fast-action dried yeast
• 300 ml whole milk, lukewarm
• 1 egg
• 40 g unsalted butter, softened

For the garlic butter:
• 100 g unsalted butter, softened
• 4 garlic cloves, finely minced
• Small handful fresh parsley, finely chopped
• ½ tsp salt
• Pinch chilli flakes (optional)

• 6 large frankfurters

Method:
1. Mix the flour, salt, sugar and yeast in a bowl. Add the milk and egg and mix into a dough.
2. Knead until smooth, then knead in the butter.
3. Cover and leave to rise until doubled.
4. Divide into 6, roll into long sausage shapes and flatten slightly.
5. Leave to rise again until puffy.
6. Bake at 180°C fan for 15–18 minutes until lightly golden.
7. Mix together all the garlic butter ingredients.
8. Lightly score the frankfurters and cook on a griddle pan until coloured.
9. Slice the buns open, spread with garlic butter and toast cut-side down until golden.
10. Add cheese and cooked bacon bits, close the buns and heat gently with a lid on until melted.
11. Add the hot dogs and top with caramelised onions, crispy onions, ketchup and mustard.
12. Serve immediately

authorMeta.name: raimeetravel
text:
If you’ll be anywhere in France on June 21… prepare yourself 😂🎶

Fête de la Musique is basically one giant nationwide music festival where the entire country turns into a party. Streets close down, random corners become dance floors, bars spill outside, DJs set up in the middle of plazas, and somehow every single person you know ends up out until sunrise.

A few things to know before you go:

• It gets VERY crowded in big cities like Paris
• A lot of people just wander from neighborhood to neighborhood following whatever music sounds best
• Wear comfy shoes because you’ll walk way more than you think
• Public transport becomes chaos late at night so plan ahead
• Phone service can get spotty with so many people out
• Bring cash + portable charger
• Dinner reservations? Good luck lol
• Some of the best music is honestly the random street performers you accidentally find! 
• My favorite area to explore for this was Montmarte! 

Also, don’t over-plan!! Pick an area, start walking, grab a drink somewhere outside, follow the music, and let the night unfold. If you have a trip here and didn’t know about this … well now you know ! I didn’t know it was a thing until I went last year and I had so much fun!

It’s one of those nights that makes Europe feel magical in the best possible way 🥹✨🇫🇷 #paris #fetedelamusique #summersolstice #europeansummer
